data.title: American Express Expands AI Payment Solutions with New Developer Toolkit
data.description:
American Express has announced an expansion into the AI payment sector with the launch of a developer toolkit aimed at 'agent-based commerce.' According to Foresight News, the toolkit is designed to support AI agents in conducting payments and other economic activities on behalf of users. It includes mechanisms such as payment credential access for verified agents and cardholder identity verification to reduce fraud and dispute risks.

American Express also highlighted its established mechanisms for handling transaction disputes, which can provide protection against errors made by AI agents. This initiative is expected to enhance user trust and increase transaction volumes. Additionally, the company has developed various plans concerning stablecoins, potentially exploring their use in settlement scenarios rather than direct payments, though specific details have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Steady Following Lower-than-Expected PPI Data
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields remained stable after the release of Producer Price Index (PPI) data, which showed a smaller increase than anticipated. According to Jin10, the PPI data suggested that inflationary pressures might be easing, which could influence the Federal Reserve's future monetary policy decisions. The stability in Treasury yields reflects investor sentiment that the Federal Reserve may not need to raise interest rates as aggressively as previously thought. This development comes amid ongoing discussions about the U.S. economic outlook and the potential impact of inflation on financial markets.

data.title: Italian Prime Minister Suspends Defense Pact with Israel Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has suspended a defense agreement with Israel that has been in place for over 20 years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this decision comes in response to the ongoing conflict with Iran, which has resulted in thousands of casualties and a significant increase in oil and gas prices. The suspension reflects Italy's strategic reassessment amid escalating tensions in the region.

data.title: Bank of Japan Governor: Economy and Prices Align with Expectations
data.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda stated that the current state of Japan's economy and prices is largely in line with the central bank's expectations. According to Jin10, Ueda's comments suggest that the Bank of Japan's monetary policy is effectively supporting the country's economic objectives. The alignment of economic indicators with the bank's forecasts indicates stability in Japan's economic environment. Ueda's remarks come amid ongoing assessments of global economic conditions and their potential impact on Japan's financial landscape.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Nominee Walsh Clears Confirmation Hurdle
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Walsh has overcome obstacles to his confirmation, according to Odaily. U.S. Senator Scott announced that Walsh's hearing is scheduled for next week, with expectations that his nomination will be approved.

data.title: Bitfinex report: Tokenized commodities market capitalization surpasses $7 billion, stablecoin infrastructure upgrades accelerate.
data.description: A report released by Bitfinex points out that tokenized commodities are moving from early experiments to practical applications, becoming a significant manifestation of the reconstruction of real-world assets (RWAs) on the blockchain. Its core is not creating new demand, but rather reshaping existing market infrastructure. Data shows that the total market capitalization of tokenized commodities has reached approximately $7 billion, growing by nearly 600% since the beginning of 2025. Current major participants include crypto-native investors and high-net-worth individuals. Against the backdrop of escalating geopolitical volatility, tokenization is improving asset liquidity and risk management flexibility. Gold remains the primary entry point, with Tether Gold holding nearly 40% market share. The report points out that on-chain gold possesses characteristics such as real-time transfer and global auditability, making it more suitable as collateral than physical gold, and can overcome traditional transaction time and settlement limitations. In addition to gold, tokenized commodities have expanded to oil, natural gas, and agricultural products, with soybeans and soybean oil each valued at approximately $400 million, and green finance-related exposure reaching approximately $850 million, demonstrating the potential for cross-category expansion. Meanwhile, blockchain's traceability enhances supply chain transparency and meets regulatory and ESG requirements. Bitfinex believes that future tokenization will shift its focus from precious metals to industrial commodities such as copper and oil, achieving a transformation from "product innovation" to "market infrastructure upgrades" by improving collateral efficiency, asset turnover speed, and transparency.

data.title: EU to Propose Measures to Address Energy Price Impact from Iran Conflict
data.description: The European Union is set to introduce a series of measures next week aimed at mitigating the impact of the Iran conflict on energy prices across the continent. Bloomberg posted on X that these initiatives are also designed to accelerate the electrification of the economy, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and preventing future energy shocks. The EU's strategy comes in response to the ongoing geopolitical tensions, which have significantly affected global energy markets. By focusing on sustainable energy solutions, the EU aims to enhance energy security and stability in the region.

data.title: American Express launches AI-powered payment agent development kit to explore stablecoin settlement application scenarios.
data.description: Payment giant American Express announced an accelerated push into AI payments and launched a developer toolkit for "Agentic Commerce," promising purchase protection against erroneous transactions generated by registered AI agents. This toolkit aims to support AI agents in completing payments and other economic activities on behalf of users, while mitigating fraud and dispute risks through mechanisms such as granting payment credentials only to verified agents and verifying cardholder identity. Furthermore, American Express stated that it already has a mature mechanism for handling transaction disputes, providing protection against AI agent errors, thereby increasing user trust and driving transaction volume growth. It also has "multiple plans" for stablecoins, reportedly exploring stablecoin settlement scenarios rather than payments, but specific details were not disclosed. (Fortune)

data.title: Bitcoin Options Show Decline in Implied Volatility Amid Market Adjustments
data.description:
Bitcoin's price has been on the rise, yet the implied volatility (IV) of major-term options is decreasing. According to Odaily, this trend is accompanied by a noticeable positive skew. The primary reason for these developments is the easing of military tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which has reduced market concerns over war risks, leading to a significant drop in the price of put options.

Trading volumes in both order books and block trades are relatively balanced, with most transactions focusing on the current and following months. The market is readjusting its position layouts, and there is a growing consensus among major participants regarding future market trends. The expectation of low volatility is becoming the mainstream outlook.

data.title: Visa, Stripe, and Zodia Custody Join Tempo Blockchain as Validators
data.description: Visa, Stripe, and Zodia Custody, a subsidiary of Standard Chartered Bank, have become validators on the Tempo blockchain. According to Odaily, these entities are now responsible for running the infrastructure that validates, orders, and finalizes transactions on the chain. Tempo recently launched on the mainnet and introduced a proxy payment protocol designed to facilitate transactions through software agents.

data.title: Open-Source Polymarket Bot Targets Non-Sports Markets
data.description: Sterling Crispin has released an open-source bot for Polymarket, designed to automatically purchase 'No' positions in non-sports markets. According to NS3.AI, Polymarket data indicates that 73.3% of resolved markets conclude with a 'No' outcome. Crispin noted that for the strategy to be effective over the long term, entry prices must be below $0.73, with the bot's maximum threshold set at $0.65.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh will testify next week, and his nomination is expected to be approved.
data.description: According to Fox Business, Federal Reserve Chair nominee Peter Warsh has cleared hurdles preventing his confirmation. Senator Sceott stated that Warsh's hearings will take place next week, and his nomination is expected to be approved. (Jinshi)

data.title: Citi Group Reports Q1 2026 Revenue of $24.6 Billion, Exceeding Expectations
data.description: Citi Group has announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026, reporting a revenue of $24.6 billion. According to Jin10, this marks an increase from the $21.6 billion recorded in the same period last year. The reported revenue also surpassed market expectations, which had anticipated $23.358 billion for the quarter. This performance highlights Citi Group's continued growth and resilience in the financial sector.

data.title: Paxos Labs raises $12 million, led by Blockchain Capital.
data.description: Paxos Labs has spun off from stablecoin issuer Paxos and raised $12 million in funding, led by Blockchain Capital, with participation from Robot Ventures, family office Maelstrom, and DeFi developer Uniswap Labs. Paxos Labs co-founder Bhaumik Kotecha declined to disclose the valuation of the round. Paxos CEO Chad Cascarilla is also the CEO of Paxos Labs. Paxos Labs will primarily provide services to large enterprises, enabling them to create and manage their own stablecoins. (Fortune)

data.title: Nearly 400 XAUt were withdrawn from the Gate hot wallet, worth approximately $1,899,100.
data.description: According to on-chain monitoring data, 399.999777 XAUt (approximately US$1,899,100) were transferred out of the Gate hot wallet.

data.title: Visa, Stripe, and Zodia Custody join Tempo blockchain validators.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that Visa, Stripe, and Zodia Custody (a subsidiary of Standard Chartered Bank) have become validators on the Tempo blockchain, responsible for running the infrastructure for validating, ordering, and finally confirming on-chain transactions. Tempo recently launched its mainnet and also introduced a proxy payment protocol designed to enable transactions through software proxies. (TheBlock)

data.title: Significant Movement of Binance Coin Observed in Recent Days
data.description: On April 14, a notable movement of Binance Coin (BNB) was detected. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, over the past three days, 15 newly created wallets have withdrawn a total of 138.26 million BNB from a centralized exchange (CEX). This activity highlights a significant shift in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: BTQ Technologies and Daou Data Collaborate on Post-Quantum Security for South Korea
data.description: BTQ Technologies, a specialist in quantum-resistant cryptography, has announced a strategic partnership with South Korean IT and payment infrastructure company Daou Data. According to Odaily, the collaboration aims to introduce a hardware-based post-quantum security architecture to South Korea's electronic payment systems. This initiative will incorporate South Korea's post-quantum cryptography (K-PQC) and hardware trust architecture to enhance the security of key generation, encryption processing, and key management. The move is designed to address potential threats posed by quantum computing, including 'collect now, decrypt later' attack models.

data.title: The suspected controller of Binance Life withdrew 50.5 million Binance Life tokens from Binance through six wallets 10 minutes ago.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, 10 minutes ago, the suspected controllers of Binance Life withdrew another 50.5 million Binance Life tokens (US$16.08 million) from Binance to the blockchain through 6 wallets. Currently, the suspected control addresses of Binance Life hold 197 million Binance Life tokens on the blockchain (19.7% of the total supply, worth US$62.58 million).

data.title: UK Chancellor Criticizes U.S. President Trump Over Middle East Conflict Impact
data.description: UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves has criticized U.S. President Donald Trump for the negative impact his Middle East conflict has had on the global economy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Reeves' accusation that Trump engaged in the conflict without a clear exit strategy. The Chancellor's remarks underscore growing international concern over the economic repercussions of the ongoing situation.
